DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-02-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ramsey Hanafi, Esq. 415-735-5285 of Quintana Hanafi for Plaintiff.
Shareef Farag, Esq. 310-820-8800 of Baker & Hostetler LLP for Defendant Fillmore Capital Partners through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Both sides address the Court's Concern. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT ERIK STRAND's DEMURRER DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANTS'CROSS COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Cross-Defendant Erik Strand's demurrer to Cross-Complainants Fillmore Capital Partners, LLC and Ronald E. Silva's cross-complaint is sustained without leave to amend. The sole cause of action for unfair business practices in violation of Business and Professions Code section 17200 et seq. fails because Cross-Complainants lack standing to bring such a claim. (see word minutes for details) (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed, (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Cross Defendant Erik Strand Demurrer To Cross Complaint (Tentative Ruling Only Part 2 Of 2)
Off Calendar Jul-02-2019 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-02-2019 Set For Trial On Dec-16-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar May-15-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Shareef S. Sarag, esq. (310-820-8800) of Baker & Hostetler, LLP for Defendants RONALD SILVA and FILLMORE CAPITAL PARTNERS, LLC via Court Call.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs RONALD SILVA and FILLMORE CAPITAL PARTNERS, LLC's Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint.

Defendants Fillmore Capital Partners, LLC and Ronald E. Silva's motion for leave to file cross-complaint is granted. California's liberal policy in favor of amendment of pleadings favors granting the motion, and Plaintiff has not shown that Defendants acted in bad faith or that he would be prejudiced by allowing the amendment. Plaintiff may raise any challenge to whether the cross-complaint states a cause of action by demurrer.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
